vol_description: These are participant recordings from the Epistemic Evaluations of Voice Assistants study, a synchronous study facilitated by recruitment and consent through Children Helping Science. In this study, children examine three different informants: a person Googling, a novel voice assistant, and a zookeeper (animal expert) on their ability to provide the right answer to questions about novel animals in multiple knowledge domains (e.g., animal health). This study is part of Phase 2 of Project GARDEN and includes children who have already participated in Phase 1 of that project.
user_affiliation: The University of Texas at Dallas
